wrapUp: The 48-team World Cup has boiled down to a Sunday final between European champions Spain and defending holders Argentina at New York New Jersey Stadium, with both semi-finals producing starkly different dramas. Spain outplayed pre-tournament favourites France 2-0 in Dallas on 14 July as Lamine Yamal won a first-half penalty converted by Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro sealed the win, while William Saliba's chronic back problem forced him off before half-time and leaves him a major doubt for Saturday's bronze medal match in Miami. Argentina staged another trademark comeback in Atlanta on 15 July, with Lionel Messi assisting Enzo Fernandez's 85th-minute equaliser and Lautaro Martinez's stoppage-time header to beat England 2-1 after Anthony Gordon had put the Three Lions ahead, extending Messi's run to four assists and eight goals in the tournament. England's late defensive reshuffle under Thomas Tuchel, including a switch to a back five that ceded 88 per cent possession after the opener, is dominating the post-mortem as the Three Lions prepare to face France for third place on 18 July. Fitness is the other headline: Yamal and Porro were cleared by Luis de la Fuente after semi-final knocks, Facundo Medina remains a calf doubt for Argentina, and Reece James is struggling with a hamstring issue for England. Opta's model now makes Spain slight favourites to lift the trophy, but Argentina have trailed in three separate knockouts and still found a way through. Each story below links to the specific report it is drawn from; squad and fitness details reflect the latest available reporting as of 16 July 2026.

sections.heading: Injuries and fitness doubts
sections.description: Players ruled out, late replacements and day-to-day concerns that could change starting line-ups and squad depth.

stories.headline: Medina races clock for Argentina final selection
stories.summary: Facundo Medina has not played since picking up a calf problem in the round-of-32 win over Cape Verde and remains a doubt for Sunday's final against Spain at New York New Jersey Stadium. The Independent reported the Nice centre-back is in a race against time to be fit, though Lionel Scaloni otherwise has a largely available squad after the draining semi-final comeback against England.

stories.headline: James doubt for England bronze medal match
stories.summary: Reece James is struggling with a potential hamstring setback after starting England's semi-final against Argentina, adding to Thomas Tuchel's problems before Saturday's third-place play-off with France in Miami. The Independent reported Declan Rice has recovered from the illness and back pain that limited him in the quarter-finals, but James's availability remains uncertain with the squad due to fly south from their Kansas base.

stories.headline: De la Fuente eases Yamal and Porro fitness fears
stories.summary: Lamine Yamal was seen limping after Spain's 2-0 semi-final win over France and Pedro Porro was substituted in the 85th minute with muscle discomfort, but Luis de la Fuente said both players escaped serious injury after speaking to the medical staff. beIN SPORTS reported Porro was dealing with muscle overload rather than a tear, and Spain's federation later confirmed the full 26-man squad was fit for selection ahead of the Argentina final.

stories.headline: Saliba forced off as back injury flares in Dallas
stories.summary: William Saliba collapsed without contact in the 30th minute of France's 2-0 semi-final defeat to Spain and was replaced by Maxence Lacroix, with reporters lip-reading him saying his back had gone after managing the problem throughout the tournament. The Athletic noted Saliba had missed France's Saturday training session before the tie, and he is now a major doubt for the third-place play-off against England in Miami on 18 July.

sections.heading: Squad selection and tactical reshuffles
sections.description: Omissions, late call-ups and manager decisions that reveal how teams plan to navigate the expanded format.

stories.headline: Messi assists late turnaround to reach final
stories.summary: Argentina came from behind to beat England 2-1 in Atlanta as Enzo Fernandez curled in an 85th-minute equaliser and substitute Lautaro Martinez headed a stoppage-time winner from Lionel Messi's cross, overturning Anthony Gordon's 55th-minute opener. FIFA's match report noted it was the first competitive meeting between the nations since 2002 and sets up a final against Spain, the country where Messi made his name with Barcelona.

stories.headline: Scaloni substitutes turn semi-final against England
stories.summary: Lionel Scaloni's second-half changes reshaped Argentina's semi-final as Nicolas Gonzalez and Rodrigo De Paul added energy before Lautaro Martinez came off the bench to score the winner. The Standard noted Martinez may now start the final ahead of Julian Alvarez, while Gonzalez and De Paul are pushing for places in a starting XI that could also feature Giuliano Simeone wide in a 4-4-2 diamond.

stories.headline: De la Fuente weighs Williams recall for final
stories.summary: Spain head into the final with a clean bill of health but Luis de la Fuente still has selection calls in wide areas and midfield, with Nico Williams pushing to start after coming off the bench in the last two ties and Pedri dropped for Rodri and Fabian Ruiz against France. FIFA's preview listed a likely XI of Simon, Porro, Laporte, Cubarsi, Cucurella, Rodri, Fabian, Yamal, Olmo, Baena and Oyarzabal for Sunday's showdown with Argentina.

stories.headline: Oyarzabal and Porro send Spain to first final since 2010
stories.summary: Mikel Oyarzabal converted a 22nd-minute penalty after Lucas Digne fouled Lamine Yamal and Pedro Porro finished a slick move with Dani Olmo for a 2-0 win over France in Dallas, Spain's third consecutive tournament victory over Les Bleus. Sky Sports reported Kylian Mbappe was shackled throughout as Luis de la Fuente's side extended their unbeaten run to 37 matches and booked a first World Cup final appearance since lifting the trophy in South Africa.

sections.heading: Disruption and preparation issues
sections.description: Travel, visa and logistical problems that could affect match readiness rather than just headlines.

stories.headline: England and France meet in Miami bronze match
stories.summary: The losing semi-finalists face each other in the Match for Third Place at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami Gardens on Saturday 18 July, kicking off at 10pm BST the day before the final in New Jersey. The Athletic noted neither England nor France wanted the fixture but FIFA retains the bronze medal match to fill the scheduling gap and provide another broadcast window, with both sides expected to rotate after draining semi-final weeks.

stories.headline: England fly to Miami after Kansas recovery base
stories.summary: England returned to their Kansas training camp after the Atlanta semi-final heartbreak and must travel to Miami for the bronze medal match 48 hours before the World Cup final kicks off in New Jersey. BBC Sport reported the squad had already played 120 minutes against Norway in the Florida heat and now face another cross-country flight before a fixture Thomas Tuchel admitted neither side truly wants to contest.

stories.headline: Finalists converge on New York New Jersey Stadium
stories.summary: Spain and Argentina have four days to prepare for the 104th match of the tournament at New York New Jersey Stadium on Sunday 19 July, with kick-off at 3pm local time. FIFA's preview noted the venue hosted Norway's shock win over Brazil in the round of 16 and will stage the first World Cup final between reigning European and South American champions, with Messi facing the country where he spent most of his club career.

sections.heading: Analysis and tournament outlook
sections.description: Deeper previews on favourites, group dynamics and how the 48-team format changes the path to the final.

stories.headline: Tuchel's defensive shift questioned after England exit
stories.summary: Thomas Tuchel replaced goalscorer Anthony Gordon with Ezri Konsa in the 72nd minute and switched to a back five as England sat on a 1-0 lead, but Argentina had 88 per cent possession for the following 37 minutes before scoring twice. BBC Sport analysis noted England are the only team this century to lead a World Cup semi-final and fail to reach the final on two separate occasions, with Tuchel insisting he had no regrets about trying to close the gaps.

stories.headline: Messi targets Golden Boot and repeat triumph
stories.summary: Lionel Messi assisted both goals in Argentina's semi-final comeback and is level with Kylian Mbappe on eight tournament goals, with four assists giving him the edge in the tie-breaker should they finish level. BBC Sport noted Messi has scored or assisted in 13 consecutive matches for club and country and would equal his career-best run of 14 with another goal contribution in Sunday's final, potentially becoming only the second player after Cafu to appear in three World Cup finals.

stories.headline: Opta makes Spain favourites for Sunday final
stories.summary: Opta Analyst reported Spain carry a 56 per cent probability of winning the World Cup after beating France, with Argentina at 44 per cent following their semi-final comeback against England. Spain arrive unbeaten in 37 competitive matches and conceded only once in seven games, while Argentina have trailed in three separate knockouts yet reached a second consecutive final, with Lionel Messi on eight goals and four assists.

stories.headline: Possession machine meets comeback kings in final
stories.summary: Spain suffocated France with long spells on the ball before striking through Yamal's penalty and Porro's finish, while Argentina needed late goals in three separate knockouts to reach the showpiece. Al Jazeera framed Sunday's final as near perfection against legacy, with 19-year-old Yamal representing Spain's future and 39-year-old Messi chasing a second consecutive World Cup and potential Golden Boot on eight goals, level with Kylian Mbappe.
